content.problemStatement:
Chef gave you a string SSS of size NNN which contains only lowercase English letters  and asked you to find the length of the longest substring of the string which satisfies the following condition:


Each character β\betaβ should appear at most f(β)f(\beta)f(β) times.
Here f(β)f(\beta)f(β) denotes the index of the character β\betaβ in the alphabet series. For example f(′a′)f('a')f(′a′) = 111, f(′b′)f('b')f(′b′) = 222 and so on.

**Note:** A substring of a string is a contiguous subsequence of that string.


Input Format

First-line will contain TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains two lines of input.
The first line of every test case contains an integer NNN - the size of the string.
The second line of every test case contains a string SSS of size NNN.


Output Format
For each testcase, output in a single line - the answer to the iii-th test case.
constraints: The string contains only lowercase english letters

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111: Every character appears less than its index in the English alphabet so we can consider the entire string.


Test Case 222: We can consider 'b' at most 2 times, therefore, the answer is 333.

solution.approach: The code uses the **sliding window** technique to find the longest valid **substring**. It maintains a frequency map to count occurrences of characters while expanding the right pointer. For each character added, it checks if any character exceeds its allowed frequency based on its position in the alphabet. If so, it adjusts the left pointer to shrink the window until the substring becomes valid again. At each valid state, it updates the **maximum** length found. This efficiently finds the longest substring meeting the character frequency condition.
